title: ranunculus ficaria subsp. calthifolius
intro: Ranunculus ficaria subsp. calthifolius, commonly known as creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the buttercup family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus ficaria subsp. calthifolius include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in creams and ointments to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
